note: site selector <-> plugin selector. The parity contract. Add a pair when a surface is ported.
expectedGapsNote: pair ids whose GAP is a KNOWN, FILED deferral (it needs a DOM/TS change, so it cannot be closed in CSS). diff.mjs downgrades these to WARN instead of GAP. Every id listed here MUST cite a workspace FOLLOWUPS.md number in this note. Empty = no deferrals outstanding.

pairs.why: card plate: gradient + bevel + hairline. The plugin side is the HOST div — pipeline.ts stamps data-dse-element on the same root theme.ts stamps data-dse-theme on, and styles-source.css puts the plate there; .dse-feature is an inner content div with no plate.
flat right-rail slot: hairline + .35em radius + small-caps, no fill/shadow

statblock plate. The site plate is .md-typeset.sb (steel-statblock.css:76) — .sb-wrap is an unstyled positioning wrapper, so pairing it monitored nothing. Plugin plate is .dse-sb (the statblock host is deliberately transparent).
featureblock plate. The site plate is .md-typeset.fb (steel-featureblock.css:44) — .fb-wrap is an unstyled positioning wrapper. Plugin plate is the HOST div; .dse-fb is the inner content div.

reference-card plate (kit/condition/ancestry/treasure/... via CardLayout). The site node is the .sc-card tile (steel-redesign.css:159-166: --fx-card-bg + --fx-bevel + lift shadow + hairline); its field grammar (__head/__name/__type/__flavor/__stats/__body) is the same one DisplayCardView emits as __title/__subtitle/__badges/__flavor/__rows/__body. Verified against the live pages: the detail pages are NOT the counterpart — a kit detail page is one .sc-kit page-plate and condition/ancestry/treasure detail pages carry no sc- card node at all — so urls.json gained the kit index (tiles) + the perk index (the .sc-card--wide variant, which only re-lays-out the same plate).
